texts.full_text: about 72,000 cultivated plants in the greenhouses showed such symptoms. To identify the causal agent, sap from leaves of the diseased plants was inoculated into Chenopodium quinoa and Nicotiana benthamiana plants. Local necrotic lesions appeared on inoculated leaves of C. quinoa, but no systemic infection was observed. Systemic mosaic symptoms were observed on the N. benthamiana plants inoculated. Single local lesion isolation was performed by using leaves from the systemically infected N. benthamiana plants. The single local lesion isolates produced similar systemic symptoms on both indicator hosts, suggesting the presence of a single virus species. An electron microscopic examination of infected leaf sap showed isometric particles that were 25–30 nm in diameter with a dense core, consistent with a potyvirus-like particle. Thus, this virus was designated the Colombian datura virus (CDV) isolate Chiba-1. The complete sequence of the

The move is expected to encourage power producers to step up generation of power. Whether the move will have much effect remains to be seen as Chinese electricity consumption is expected to pick up in June and remain high for the rest of the summer. The government is reporting that domestic fuel stocks are falling as more industries are turning on diesel-fired generators to keep production lines moving. The State Grid Corporation predicted that this summer's power shortage will be worse than in 2004. The drought in Central China is now said to be the worst in 60 years, and in Hunan province the papers are calling the situation the worst in 100 years. In several provinces there is inadequate drinking water and river traffic has been suspended. In 30 percent of Hunan's reservoirs the water level is too low for irrigation and some 700,000 hectares of farmland have been affected. On Saturday, China's Premier, Wen Jiabao, presided over an anti-drought seminar and called for stronger efforts to combat the situation. Despite the dire pronouncements coming out of some of the key agricultural regions, China's Agriculture Ministger Han Chjangfu announced that China will have a bumper harvest of wheat and rapeseed this summer. The paint() function in QGraphicsItem is automatically called by the QGraphicsView when the item needs to be redrawn. This can happen due to various reasons, such as the item being moved, resized, or the item being in the viewable area of the QGraphicsView. The paint() function is called approximately every 100 ms because the QGraphicsView has a default frame rate of around 10 Hz, which can be adjusted by changing the view's frameRate(). texts.full_text: e domain Archaea or specific heat-resistant bacteria like Thermus aquaticus and Sulfolobales. The study of these geobiological systems involves understanding how microorganisms interact with minerals within rocks (biomineralization) and influence geochemical processes, such as elemental cycling in extreme environments. Endoliths can alter rock porosity through their metabolic activities by dissolving or precipitating elements like silica, iron oxides/hydroxides, calcium carbonates among others. In the Yellowstone geothermal system specifically: 1. Microbial Diversity: Researchers have identified a wide range of archaea and bacteria that thrive in these endolithic environments. 2. Environmental Factors Impacting Microbes: - Temperature: The thermal gradient from cool to hyperthermal (upwards of 90°C) influences microbial community composition, with specific taxa dominating at certain temperature ranges. - pH Levels: Highly acidic conditions are common near hot springs and geysers in Yellowstone; microbes here must be acidophiles or extremophile bacteria that can tolerate such low-pH environments. 3. texts.full_text: v. KING et al. Supreme Court of Florida, Special Division B. July 18, 1952. Rehearing Denied October 14, 1952. Hall & Farnsworth, Tampa, for appellants. Lewis W. Petteway, Tallahassee, for Florida R. & Public Utilities Commission. John M. Allison of Macfarlane, Ferguson, Allison & Kelly, Tampa, for Central Truck Lines, Inc. Milam, McIlvaine, Carroll & Wattles and Wayne K. Ramsey, Jr., Jacksonville, for Great Southern Trucking Co., appellees. PER CURIAM. Affirmed. SEBRING, C.J., and ROBERTS and MATHEWS, JJ., and FUTCH, Associate Justice, concur. On Rehearing. ROBERTS, Justice. This is an appeal to review a final decree of the Circuit Court of Hillsborough County, in Chancery. The suit was prosecuted by Bruce's Juices, Inc., together with certain intervenors joining later, against the Florida Railroad & Public Utilities Commission and others for the purpose of enjoining enforcement of two orders entered by said commission granting to Central Truck Lines, Inc., authority as a common carrier over specified routes. The grounds alleged in support of such injunction were that (1) there was no competent substantial evidence before the commission upon which it could base its finding "that public convenience and necessity required" operation under certificates granted; (2) because appellants had not been given adequate notice, by publication or otherwise, as to application for said certificate so they might appear at hearing held on such applications. After careful consideration of briefs filed here we find no merit in either contention made by appellant — that is: there was ample evidence before commission from which it could have concluded "public convenience and necessity required" operation under certificates granted; also, appellants were given sufficient notice as to pendency of application for certificate so they might appear at hearing held on such applications. Accordingly, the decree appealed must be affirmed — but without prejudice to right in any party aggrieved by operations authorized hereinunder to apply anew and secure further administrative review thereof before said commission or through appropriate proceedings under law had subsequent thereto if desired; provided however that no rights here granted shall operate retroactively nor affect existing contracts between parties hereto.

In Group II, 12 patients (12%) showed signs of endometrial polyp recurrence. The difference in recurrence rates between the two groups was statistically significant (p<0.05). Discussion: Hysteroscopic polypectomy appears to be more than fractionated curettage in preventing the recurrence of endometrial polyps. This may be due to the precise removal of polyps and the ability to visualize the uterine cavity completely during hysteroscopy. Further studies with larger sample sizes are needed to confirm these findings. Conclusion: Hysteroscopic polypectomy is a safe and effective method for treating endometrial polyps and may reduce the risk of recurrence compared to fractionated curettage. Patients with a history of endometrial polyps should be closely monitored for recurrence, regardless of the initial treatment method used. Another important observation is that in all the three cases of sterno-bronchial fistulae, *S. aureus* was cultured and was part of the etiology, and that although the peristernal tissue was involved, the sternal bone remained intact.\[[@ref2]\] The removal of the pacing wires under local anesthesia can itself leave behind a communication between the pericardium and bronchial tree, which may lead to further complications and must be considered when dealing with patients who are at a higher risk and cannot undergo a radical operation. All the three cases showed an element of infection with the fistulae and a good recovery when the patients were treated up to the degree of follow-up allowed by the patient, although uncommon sterno-bronchial fistulae can occur and they can easily be missed if a fistulography is not performed. Treating an infected fistula with antibiotics may slow down the process of infection, but will not fix the fistula, which will require an operation. In patients who are generally stable, removal of the wires, debridement, and a course of antibiotics may resolve a problem which could potentially be life-threatening if not accounted for and treated. To conclude, sterno-bronchial fistulae formed due to retained pacing wires after a cardiac operation are relatively rare complications, which do, however, occur and may not be initially diagnosed, prolonging patient deterioration. Cholecystectomy, one of the most common abdominal surgical procedures, has sparked a heated debate among surgeons in western France regarding the routine practice of intraoperative cholangiography (IOC). Despite the lack of a formal agreement, a survey of 300 visceral surgeons in the region revealed valuable insights into their practices and opinions. Of the 148 interpretable answers, 125 (83.4%) surgeons routinely perform IOC (IOCr group), while 23 (15.4%) opt for selective IOC (IOCs group). The mean age of responding surgeons was 49.3 years. texts.full_text: iles of wreckage. The silverIn this harrowing account from "The Mountain Runners," the perilous journey undertaken by participants in Mount Baker Marathon is vividly brought to life. First-time runners who chose Deming's trailhead faced a formidable challenge as they embarked on their high-speed car trip. The vehicles that transported them were often crude and unreliable, with thin metal skins hiding little more than mechanical skeletons beneath the surface of each vehicle—a stark contrast from today’s well-engineered automobile safety standards. The journey itself was an intense test against both nature's unforgiving terrain as much human endurance limits. At speeds that would be considered reckless by modern drivers and runners, these competitors braced themselves for potential accidents or mechanical failures at any moment during their 65 mph drive through the mountainside roads—a fact underscored tragically in this particular race when one of those vehicles succumbed to its own flaws. The Glacier trailhead presented a different kind but equally perilous challenge. The steam engine that hauled runners there was an antiquated and unwieldy beast, compared with the sleeker engines we are accustomed today—and it carried them through rugged mountain landscapes at speeds far beyond what most modern trains would be comfortable or safe doing. October 28, 2005|Chris Pasles | Times Staff Writer A nine-hour marathon of Olivier Messiaen's complete organ music may look like a stunt to some people. But Paul Jacobs is deadly serious about playing all the 20th century French composer's organ works Saturday at the Cathedral of Our Lady of the Angels. "We're living in a cynical and jaded age, and Messiaen is the antithesis ofIn a world often marked by cynicism and jadedness, the music of Olivier Messiaen stands as a beacon of hope and transcendence. Paul Jacobs, the organist from the Juilliard School, is set to perform a nine-hour marathon of Messiaen's complete organ works at the Cathedral of Our Lady of the Angels, a venue whose acoustics and grandeur perfectly complement the composer's monumental compositions. Jacobs' dedication to this endeavor is not merely a technical feat but a profound act of musical advocacy, aimed at conveying the composer's message of joy and faith. Messiaen's organ works, known for their intricate harmonies and spiritual depth, are a testament to the composer's Catholic faith and his belief in the divine. I think Battlefront has a vast variety of Iconic Star Wars heroes such as Luke Skywalker, Darth Vader, Kylo Ren, Rey, Yoda and more, a short single-player campaign, beautiful and awesome locations and upgraded Blasters. Some people don’t like the short single-pplayer campaign or the inclusion of microtransactions for cosmetic items and loot boxes that affected gameplay balance. However, despite these criticisms, Star Wars Battlefront II has received numerous updates and improvements since its launch, addressing many of the initial concerns. ### Key Points: - Single-Player Campaign: While it's brief, the campaign offers a compelling story set between and , providing new insights into the Star Wars universe. - Multiplayer Modes: The game features a wide array of multiplayer modes, including large-scale battles with up to 40 players, space combat, and heroic battles where you can play as your favorite characters. - Character Variety: As mentioned, the roster includes iconic heroes and villains from across the Star Wars saga, each with unique abilities that can turn the tide of battle. - Visuals and Sound: The game boasts stunning visuals and a soundtrack that captures the essence of the films, immersing players in the Star Wars universe. - Post-Launch Support: EA DICE has continued to support the game with regular updates, new content, and balance adjustments, making it a more polished experience over time. ### Community Reception: The initial backlash was significant, but many fans have since returned to the game after the removal of microtransactions for gameplay advantages. The community remains active, and the game continues to offer a rich and engaging Star Wars experience. What are your thoughts on Star Wars Battlefront II? This structure exhibits excellent scalability and manufacturing simplicity, making it suitable for use in modern integrated circuit applications. Memory cell structures based on charge trapping dielectric layers include structures known by the industry name PHINES, for example. These memory cell structures store data by trapping charge in a charge trapping dielectric layer, such as silicon nitride. As negative charge is trapped, the threshold voltage of the memory cell increases. The threshold voltage of the memory cell is reduced by removing negative charge from the charge trapping layer. texts.full_text: acute coronary syndrome (ACS), which encompasses conditions like unstable angina and heart attacks (myocardial infarction). These conditions often result from blood clots in the coronary arteries, which can block blood flow and lead to heart damage. Tenecteplase works by breaking down these clots, restoring blood flow, and reducing heart damage. Unlike other fibrinolytics, it has a longer half-life, allowing a single IV dose, which is advantageous in emergency settings. It is particularly effective in cases of STEMI, where timely administration can improve patient outcomes by restoring blood flow and reducing heart damage. texts.full_text: s of all sizes wIth measurable results through a growing fitness network. We have found if workers who exercise at the gym at least 12 times a month are reimbursed a mere $20 a month on their fitness club membership fees, they tend to exercise three times as often as those who do not participate in the reimbursement programs. Similarly, workers who receive the reimbursements are twice as likely to renew their fitness club memberships at the end of a 12-month contract as those who do not receive the reimbursements. The benefit to employees is clear, but the value for employers is also tangible: When employees exercise more often and eat better, morale improves, productivity increases and health care costs go down. Healthy Contributions is based in Minnesota, where six of the largest health benefits providers offer the $20 monthly fitness incentives to their covered members.
